data.title: Ventia Is Tuatahi's Primary Service Partner for Fiber Build, Connections, Maintenance
Western Region Gold to Issue Bonds of Up to 2 Billion Yuan; Shares Soar 10%
data.description:
Western Region Gold proposed to issue bonds amounting to 2 billion yuan.

The Chinese gold smelting company will issue medium-term bonds of up to 1 billion yuan and corporate bonds of the same amount, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The proceeds from the issuance will be used to repay debt, supplement working capital, and support project construction, the filing said.

The plan is still subject to shareholders' and regulators' approvals.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Posiflex Technology's Chairman Steps Down
data.description:
Posiflex Technology's chairman, Chieh-Wen Cheng, resigned on Thursday, citing personal career planning as the reason for his departure.

The company has not appointed a successor yet, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Shares of the self-service technology solutions provider dropped over 3% recently.

data.title: Indian shares tumble at the open, tracking Asian peers, after Israel strikes Iran
data.description:
Indian equities fell at the open on Friday, tracking declines in Asian markets, after Israel launched military strikes on Iran, escalating tensions in the oil-rich Middle East.

The Nifty 50 was down 1.67% at 24,473 and the BSE Sensex slid 1.55% to 80,427.81, as of 9:15 a.m. IST.

All 13 sectors logged losses. The broader, more domestically focussed smallcaps and midcaps shed 2% each.

The MSCI Asia ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 1.1%, while safe havens like gold and the Swiss franc gained and oil prices <LCOc1> surged 9% amid fears of a supply disruption.

Israel said it struck Iranian nuclear targets to block Tehran from developing atomic weapons. The U.S. has ruled out any involvement in the strikes against Iran.

($1 = 85.5530 Indian rupees)

data.title: Ventia Has Been a Strategic Partner to New Zealand's Tuatahi Since 2011
Bermaz Auto Target Price Lowered to MYR0.86 from MYR0.95 by RHB IB >5248.KU
Grab Prices Upsized Offering of $1.5 Billion Bonds
data.description:
Singapore-based Grab upsized its offering of $1.25 billion worth of convertible senior bonds due 2030 to $1.5 billion, according to a company release on Tuesday.

Proceeds raised from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes, to optimize strategic flexibility and to fund potential acquisitions.

The bonds will mature on June 15, 2030, and will not bear a regular interest.

data.title: Rescuers search for missing people, aircraft parts after Air India crash kills over 240
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dreamliner crash is first for Boeing's wide-body airliner
  • Sole survivor of flight in hospital
  • Local media says deaths on ground could be as high as 24

Rescue workers searched for missing people and aircraft parts on Friday after an Air India plane crashed onto a medical college hostel in the city of Ahmedabad, killing more than 240 people in the world's worst aviation disaster in a decade.

The Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner with 242 people on board, headed for Gatwick Airport south of London, had only one survivor after it crashed onto the hostel during lunch hour.

There were deaths on the ground as well, with local media putting the toll as high as 24. Reuters could not immediately verify the number. Authorities said they were still searching for people missing on the ground.

Rescue personnel continued their search through the night and early morning, hunting for missing aircraft parts amid the debris that could explain why the plane crashed soon after taking off.

Local newspaper Hindustan Times reported that one of two black boxes from the plane had been found. Reuters could not verify the report and the paper did not say whether the flight data recorder or the cockpit voice recorder had been recovered.

Residents living in the vicinity said that construction of the hostel for resident doctors was completed only a year ago and the buildings were not fully occupied.

"We were at home and heard a massive sound, it appeared like a big blast. We then saw very dark smoke which engulfed the entire area," said 63-year-old Nitin Joshi, who has been living in the area for more than 50 years.

CCTV footage showed the plane taking off over a residential area and then disappearing from the screen before a huge fireball could be seen rising into the sky from beyond the houses.

Parts of the plane's fuselage were scattered around the smouldering building into which it crashed. The tail of the plane was stuck on top of the building.

Air India said the lone survivor, a British national, was being treated in the hospital.

The man told Indian media how he had heard a loud noise shortly after Flight AI171 took off.

Vidhi Chaudhary, a top state police officer, said on Thursday the death toll was more than 240, revising down a previous toll of 294 as it included body parts that had been double counted.

The dead included Vijay Rupani, the former chief minister of Gujarat state, of which Ahmedabad is the main city.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who hails from Gujarat, has visited the site and Air India Chief Executive Officer Campbell Wilson also arrived in the early hours of Friday.

The company has said the investigation would take time. Planemaker Boeing has said a team of experts is ready to go to India to help in the probe.

It was the first crash for the Dreamliner, a wide-body airliner that began flying commercially in 2011, according to the Aviation Safety Network database.

The plane that crashed on Thursday flew for the first time in 2013 and was delivered to Air India in January 2014, Flightradar24 said.

The last fatal plane crash in India, the world's third-largest aviation market and its fastest growing, was in 2020 and involved Air India Express, the airline's low-cost arm.

The formerly state-owned Air India was taken over by Indian conglomerate Tata Group in 2022, and merged with Vistara - a joint venture between the group and Singapore Airlines – in 2024.

data.title: Singapore Airlines' Scoot to take over Jetstar routes
data.description:
Flag carrier Singapore Airlines' budget arm, Scoot, will take over some of the routes run by Qantas Airways' Jetstar Asia, which announced this week that it would close, a Singapore Airlines spokesperson said on Friday.

Qantas announced on Wednesday that it will close its Singapore-based budget subsidiary on July 31, citing rising supplier costs, high airport fees and strong regional competition.

Subject to regulatory approval and talks with partners, Scoot will launch new flights from Singapore to Japan's Okinawa and Labuan Bajo in Indonesia, the spokesperson said. Both routes were exclusively operated by Jetstar Asia.

Singapore Airlines will also ramp up flights to other affected routes, including ones from Singapore to Manila, Colombo and Jakarta. Full flight schedules will be made public in due course, the spokesperson said.

Singapore Airlines previously said that it had set up channels for Jetstar Asia staff to expedite applications for jobs within the airline group.

data.title: Scale AI Gets Meta Investment That Values It at More Than $29 Billion
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Scale AI has been valued at more than $29 billion after a significant investment from Meta Platforms that will see its founder, Alexandr Wang, join the social-media giant to work on its AI efforts.

The artificial-intelligence startup announced the investment on Thursday, confirming an earlier report by The Wall Street Journal.

The data-labeling company said proceeds from the investment will be distributed to its shareholders, accelerate innovation efforts and strengthen existing customer relationships. Meta will hold a minority of Scale's outstanding equity.

Scale AI didn't disclose the size of the investment, but the AI startup was valued at nearly $14 billion after a fundraising round last year.

The deal marks one of Meta's largest-ever outside investments and shows the dramatic steps it is taking to revamp its AI work, which has suffered setbacks including the delayed release of a major new model.

The Wall Street Journal on Tuesday said Meta was seeking to invest around $14 billion in Scale AI, which would give it a 49% stake in the company.

The agreement will substantially expand Scale AI and Meta's commercial relationship to accelerate the deployment of Scale's data solutions, the startup said.

"We will deepen the work we do together producing data for AI models, and Alexandr Wang will join Meta to work on our superintelligence efforts. We will share more about this effort and the great people joining this team in the coming weeks," a Meta spokesman said.

Wang will remain a director on Scale AI's board. Chief Strategy Officer Jason Droege, who joined Scale in September, will serve as interim chief executive.

The nine-year-old San Francisco-based startup manages more than 100,000 contractors from across the world who label images, write sentences and type out stories that help AI chatbots learn how to think and speak like humans.

The startup has sold its services to OpenAI, Google and Meta and has more recently begun building software products that help businesses create AI tools, although this is a smaller part of its business.

Before joining Scale AI, Droege was a venture partner at Benchmark, an investment firm focused on early-stage venture investing, and was a founder of Uber Eats, Uber's food-delivery platform.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Yuexiu Property Signs HK$1.56 Billion Sustainability-Linked Loan Facility
data.description:
Yuexiu Property Company entered into a three-year HK$1.56 billion sustainability-linked term loan facility with a bank, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

An event of default would occur if controlling shareholder Yue Xiu's stake falls below 30%, if it ceases to be the single largest beneficial shareholder, or if it loses effective management control, the filing said.

data.title: Jingjin Equipment's Chairman Detained for Investigation; Shares Slump 8%
data.description:
Authorities in China's Baode County detained Jingjin Equipment's chairman and general manager, Jiang Guiting, for an investigation, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

The Chinese environmental protection service provider said the matter is not expected to have a significant impact on its normal production and operations.

The company's shares fell less than 8% in recent trade.

data.title: Indian airline stocks drop after fatal Air India crash
data.description:
** Shares of Indian airlines drop after fatal Air India plane crash

** IndiGo parent Interglobe Aviation and SpiceJet both down 4% in early trade

** More than 240 people were killed when an Air India plane bound for London crashed moments after taking off from the city of Ahmedabad on Thursday, authorities said, in the world's worst aviation disaster in a decade.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0341 GMT - Further investigations will be needed to determine the cause of the Air India crash, says Morningstar analyst Nicolas Owens in a research note. Owens expects multiple investigations to follow the deadly incident. "Fatal aircraft failures are extremely rare, but no less tragic and alarming when they occur," he says. Investigations typically take several months and may result in recommendations to improve safety practices across aircraft manufacturing, equipment standards, operations and maintenance. Morningstar maintains a fair value estimate of US$242.00 for Boeing, which last traded at US$203.75. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - LG Chem's shares could get support from a potential sale of its stake in its battery-making affiliate, says Nomura analyst Cindy Park in a note. LG Chem owns a controlling 82% stake in LG Energy Solution with a market cap of around KRW68 trillion, Park says. Any announcement or hints to sell some of the stake could be a re-rating catalyst for LG Chem. She expects LG Chem's ongoing work on manganese-rich nickel cathode to result in a 30% cost reduction in the next two years. Nomura raises the stock's target price by 7.1% to KRW300,000 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 1.2% lower at KRW208,000. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0301 GMT - XPeng is entering a strong business cycle, say Nomura analysts in a note. The company is likely to see improving shipments, higher average selling prices and stronger margins ahead. The company unveiled its first new model this year, a mid-size SUV model G7, using advanced AI technology. The new model's launch could be a good start for the company's strong business cycle, thanks to its competitive technology and family-friendly feature of an ultra-large interior space, Nomura says. The new model's features and the pre-sale price has potential for a positive surprise when the official price is released, it adds. The brokerage maintains a buy rating for Xpeng's ADR at $30.00. The ADRs last closed at $19.23. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0237 GMT - Asian aviation stocks fall, tracking overnight losses in Boeing shares, after an Air India passenger jet carrying 242 people crashed in western India on Thursday, killing all but one person. Shares in Malaysia's Capital A and AirAsia X are down 4.4% and 2.9%, respectively. Hong Kong-listed Cathay Pacific, China Southern Airlines and Air China lose 2.1%, 1.8% and 0.9%, respectively. Singapore Airlines falls 1.6% and SATS is down 1.6%. The crash marks the first fatal incident involving a Boeing 787, raising fresh doubts over the aircraft manufacturer's safety issues. Korean Air Lines is 3.1% lower and Jeju Air sheds 2.7%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0236 GMT - Perseus Mining's five-year plan is a mixed bag for investors, with production 9% higher than consensus, while costs and capex are also above market expectations, Macquarie analysts say. Its average all-in sustaining cost estimate for the period is 7% above the Visible Alpha consensus, while expected growth capex is 40% higher, say the analysts. Still, the higher forecast output prompts Macquarie to raise its target on Perseus by roughly 5% to A$4.00/share. The bank reiterates its outperform call. "Life extensions to Edikan and Sissingué are key to the stronger production, but attention now turns to the timeliness of Nyanzaga's development," the analysts say. Perseus is up 1.6% at A$3.82/share, as gold stocks rally following Israel's attack on Iran. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0206 GMT - Chinese defense stocks are broadly higher after Israel launched an attack on Iran's nuclear program overnight and declared a state of emergency ahead of potential retaliation. China Aerospace Times Electronics is up 2.85%, China CSSC Holdings gains 1.25% and CSSC Offshore & Marine Engineering is 2.0% higher. North Navigation Control Technology adds 0.7% and Continental Aerospace Technologies Holding gains 0.8%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is down 0.3% to 3393.90 and the Hang Seng Index is up 0.2%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0155 GMT - Malaysia's glove sector appears to be facing a tougher road ahead, Maybank IB analyst Wong Wei Sum writes in a note. Latest industry sources suggest that the sector's competition could intensify further, she says. By the end of this year, new capacity from a major China glove maker is expected to come online, she adds. A price war also seems to be shaping up in an oversupplied gloves market. Maybank IB maintains its negative rating on Malaysia's glove sector and maintains its sell rating on Hartalega Holdings, Kossan Rubber Industries and Top Glove.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0154 GMT - Australian banks still have some temporary tailwinds that could delay cuts to their dividends, Macquarie analysts say in a note. They believe that ANZ, Commonwealth Bank, National Australia Bank and Westpac will all have to cut payouts at some point due to weaker organic capital generation, a weak earnings outlook and a potential increase to regulatory capital buffers. However, the analysts say that Commonwealth might be able to postpone the pain for investors by divesting its stake in payments platform Klarna. They add that ANZ also has stakes in Asian banks, while regulators should soon ease Westpac's additional capital requirement. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0141 GMT - Superloop's bull at Citi looks past the end of the Australian internet-access provider's current year and sees accelerating momentum in both its consumer and wholesale operations. Analyst William Park cites factors including the ramp-up of Superloop's contract to provide branded access to power retailer Origin Energy. He tells clients in a note that feedback from Origin points to continuing momentum in customer additions. Park also thinks that competitive pricing for higher-speed tiers could underpin market-share gains as consumers progressively migrate to such plans. Park raises his Ebitda forecasts by 1% for FY 2026 and by 5% for FY 2027. Citi raises its target price 26% to A$3.35 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are down 0.7% at A$2.85. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0130 GMT - Accent Group keeps its bull at Bell Potter despite the footwear retailer's weaker-than-expected trading update. Analyst Chami Ratnapala lowers her net profit forecasts for the next three fiscal years by between 17% and 20% on what she says was an 18% gap between Accent's FY25 EBIT guidance and the average analyst forecast. However, she continues to see Reserve Bank of Australia rate cuts supporting a demand recovery toward the end of this calendar year. She also reminds clients in a note that Accent's partnership with U.K.-based Frasers Group will expose the Australian company to leading brands including Nike. Bell Potter cuts its target price 19% to A$2.10 but retains a buy rating. Shares are down 21% at A$1.42. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0129 GMT - Stocks of ASX-listed gold miners surge after Israel carried out a strike on Iran. Shares in Northern Star are up by roughly 5.1%. Newmont shares are 5.7% higher. Both are trading around a seven-week high. Spot gold is 1.1% higher, around US$3,424/troy ounce, as the strike fuels demand for bullion as a haven. The strike is also driving Australian energy stocks higher, while the ASX/S&P 200 is down 0.2% overall. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0122 GMT - Samsung Electronics is expected to report a sequential earnings fall for 2Q, Hanwha Investment & Securities analyst Kim Kwang-jin says in a note. The South Korean tech giant's operating profit is projected to drop 8% on quarter for the April-June period. Earnings growth seems to be limited by a delay in qualifying its advanced high-bandwidth-memory chips and turning around its unprofitable foundry unit as well as the negative impact of the dollar's weakness against the won, Kim notes. Still, he expects Samsung's earnings to recover from 2H, largely on higher prices for conventional DRAM chips and a possible supply of 12-layer HBM3E chips to Nvidia. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.description: Further investigations will be needed to determine the cause of the Air India crash, says Morningstar analyst Nicolas Owens in a research note. Owens expects multiple investigations to follow the deadly incident. "Fatal aircraft failures are extremely rare, but no less tragic and alarming when they occur," he says. Investigations typically take several months and may result in recommendations to improve safety practices across aircraft manufacturing, equipment standards, operations and maintenance. data.title: Oil Prices Surge After Israel Strike on Iran — Update
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui

Oil prices surged Friday after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear facilities, rattling markets and sending investors fleeing into safe-haven assets.

Israel said the attack targeted Iran's nuclear program and other military sites, as well as Iranian military commanders and nuclear scientists. Dozens of planes wrapped up the first wave of the attack before dawn Friday, Israel said.

Front-month WTI crude oil futures jumped 11% to $75.28 a barrel and front-month Brent crude oil futures climbed 10% to $76.35 per barrel.

"The announcement of Israeli airstrikes on Iran has reignited fears of a broader regional conflict, driving oil prices higher on potential supply disruption risks," said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Markets.

Safe-haven assets such as gold, government bonds, the Japanese yen and the Swiss franc also strengthened as the attack spurred investors to pare down risk.

Spot gold rose 1.6% to $3,438.89 per troy ounce.

The 10-year yield on U.S. Treasurys fell 2 basis points to 4.3332%, while that on Japanese government bonds dropped 4.5 basis points to 1.410%, and the 10-year yield on Australian sovereign securities declined 8 basis points to 4.1540%.

The dollar edged down 0.2% to 144.19 yen and was 0.3% lower at 0.8078 against the franc.

Most Asian equity markets weakened, with the Nikkei Stock Average losing 1.3% and South Korea's Kospi dropping 1.1%.

"Safe-haven assets like gold and the yen are catching a bid, while equities are under pressure as investors cut risk ahead of the weekend," Chanana added.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com

Australian energy stocks near four-month high as oil surges on Middle East tensions

data.title: Market Chatter: LG Electronics' Home Appliance Subscriptions to Surpass 2 Trillion Won in 2025; Shares Decline 4%
data.description:
LG Electronics is set to surpass 2 trillion won in annual revenue from its subscription-based home appliance business this year, Pulse News reported Thursday.

First-quarter sales, which excluded care services, jumped 45% year on year to 501 billion won, driven by strong demand for maintenance of high-use appliances such as water purifiers and washing machines, the report said.

The electronics products maker aims to evolve the model into a lifecycle business, expanding beyond rentals to include recurring sales of filters, detergents, and parts, it said.

Shares of LG Electronics fell 4% in recent trade on Friday.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Spanish Bank Sets New MREL Targets for BBVA
data.description:
Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria said Thursday it received an updated set of minimum requirements for own funds and eligible liabilities, or MREL, from the Bank of Spain.

The bank must maintain a volume of own funds and eligible liabilities at 23.13% of total risk-weighted assets of its resolution group on a sub-consolidated level, including a subordination component of 13.50% of these assets.

In addition, the bank's total requirement for own funds and eligible liabilities is 8.59% of its leverage ratio exposure, of which 5.66% must be satisfied with subordinated instruments.

The requirements are based on financial and supervisory information as of Dec. 31, 2023, and are applicable upon the receipt.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Dips 4% After Israeli Strikes on Iran Spark Global Selloff
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Bitcoin dropped over 4% as Israeli airstrikes on Iran sparked a broad selloff.
  • The U.S. began evacuations from Iraq amid fears of escalating regional conflict.
  • Crypto markets turned risk-off, with Ethereum and XRP also posting sharp losses.

Bitcoin fell sharply late Thursday, sliding over 4% to $103,556 following Israeli airstrikes on Iran that intensified already fragile Middle East tensions.

The drop from a 24-hour high of $108,500 came as investors reacted to the prospect of a broader geopolitical crisis, triggering a retreat from risk assets. The sharp sell-off led to the liquidation of $427 million in long positions in the past 24 hours.

targeted military infrastructure near Tehran and Tabriz, describing the action as a preemptive measure in response to Iran’s nuclear threat.Bitcoin Drops as Israel’s Attack on Iran Could Continue for Days

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the operation a necessary step to “remove this threat,” adding it would continue “as many days as it takes.”

Iran has yet to issue an official response, but state media reported explosions and airspace closures.

Meanwhile, the U.S. began pulling diplomats from Iraq and offered voluntary evacuations to military families in neighboring countries.

The State Department also issued warnings for American citizens to leave Iraq, citing regional instability.

In Washington, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Israel acted unilaterally but had informed the U.S. beforehand.

Former Trump advisor Steve Witkoff noted that nuclear negotiations with Iran are still on the table, though risks of escalation remain.

Historically, Bitcoin has shown sensitivity to geopolitical unrest, often falling as traders reduce exposure to volatile assets.

While the crypto asset is seen by some as a long-term hedge, its short-term price often mirrors broader risk sentiment.

Ethereum followed the downward trend, slipping below $2,500, while XRP retreated to $2.10, adding to the market-wide pressure.Bitcoin Struggles to Hold $104K Amid Tensions

Bitcoin is trading at $103,990 at the time of writing, showing signs of stabilization after a sharp 4% drop triggered by rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Price briefly dipped below $103,000 following Israeli airstrikes on Iran but has since bounced modestly.

On the 2-hour chart, BTC remains under pressure, with Bollinger Bands widening and price hugging the lower band—a sign of increased volatility and bearish momentum.

Bitcoin Just Lost Its Breakout — Here’s the Support Level That Matters NowWhy this report mattersBitcoin’s breakout above $106,000 didn’t hold, and that could mean more than just a failed rally. Ethereum’s funding rate has quietly collapsed, even as open interest surged… — 10x Research (@10x_Research)

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) has dropped to 25.51, deep in oversold territory, indicating potential for a short-term relief bounce. However, MACD values remain heavily negative (-438.59 MACD line), suggesting bearish momentum is still dominant.

Zooming into the 30-minute timeframe, price is struggling to reclaim the $105,000 zone. RSI stands at 32.30, also near oversold levels, while MACD shows downward expansion, further reinforcing downside risk if support fails.

Bollinger Bands on this timeframe show a bearish squeeze breaking to the downside.

The 1-minute chart reflects short-term recovery attempts, with RSI bouncing to 66.49 and MACD crossing bullishly. Still, this minor uptick is not yet confirmed by higher timeframes, suggesting caution is warranted.

Key levels to watch include immediate support at $102,533 and resistance at $105,693.

A close above $105,000 could ease selling pressure, but failure to reclaim it may open the door to further losses toward $100K.

For now, sentiment remains risk-off, and BTC bulls must defend current levels to avoid deeper correction.

data.title: Kuo Yang Construction Unit Awards NT$1.8 Billion Land Decoration Project
data.description:
Kuo Yang Construction unit Jiyang Construction and Development awarded a land decoration project worth NT$1.8 billion to Junjie Engineering Construction for eight parcels of land in New Taipei City, Taiwan.

Kuoyang said the project aims to develop residential buildings for sale, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchnage filing.

data.title: Ventia Secures Five-Year, A$100 Mln Contract With Tuatahi First Fibre
Shares of Woodside Energy, Santos Jump as Crude Oil Futures Surge
data.description:
Woodside Energy Group' shares surged nearly 7%, while those of Santos jumped 4% in recent trading on Friday, with crude oil futures surging over 13%.

The crude oil futures were on track for their biggest single-day gains since May 2020 after Israel launched air strikes against Iran. Brent crude oil futures rose to $78.50 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures jumped to $77.50 per barrel.

data.title: Indian gold futures breach key 100,000 rupees level to hit record high
data.description:
Indian gold futures rose above the psychologically important 100,000 rupees ($1,161.02) per 10 grams mark for the first time ever on Friday morning, on a weak rupee and tracking gains in overseas markets, dealer said.

Domestic gold futures (MAUc1) rose 2% to a record high of 100,403 rupees per 10 grams in early trade, squeezing demand in the world's second-biggest consumer of the precious metal, dealers said.

($1 = 86.1310 Indian rupees)

data.title: DayOne Data Centres Singapore Secures MYR15 Billion Financing for Green Data Centers in Malaysia
data.description:
DayOne Data Centres Singapore secured multicurrency financing of 15 billion Malaysian ringgit for its green data centers in Johor, according to a release by Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. or OCBC's Malaysian unit on Thursday.

OCBC and OCBC Bank Malaysia are the joint coordinators for the financing which comprises $1.7 billion in offshore term-loan facility and 7.5 billion ringgit in Islamic financing.

Proceeds from the financing will be used for refinancing and capital expenditure of data centers focused at obtaining green digital infrastructure certification.

data.title: Baguio Green Ends Acquisition Talks as Exclusivity Period Expires
data.description:
Baguio Green Group said it has terminated discussions for a potential acquisition after the exclusivity period lapsed without a definitive sale and purchase agreement, according to a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The talks were originally disclosed in a Jan. 13 announcement. The company said it will continue to explore growth opportunities.

data.title: LS Electric, Spain's Power Electronics Sign Deal to Boost Global ESS Equipment Supply
data.description:
LS Electric signed a memorandum of understanding with Spain-based Power Electronics to collaborate on supplying power equipment, including large-capacity transformers, for global energy storage system (ESS) projects, LS Electric said in a Thursday release.

The agreement aims to jointly develop power conversion system-optimized equipment and raise competitiveness in fast-growing renewable energy markets such as North America and Asia, the release said.

Shares of LS Electric fell more than 2% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: Palm tracks rival oils, crude higher; set for fifth weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Friday, and were on track for their fifth straight weekly gains, tracking rival edible oils at Dalian and Chicago markets as well as crude oil.

The benchmark palm oil contract for August delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 101 ringgit, or 2.63%, to 3,940 ringgit ($929.25) a metric ton by 0254 GMT.

The contract has gained 0.36% so far this week.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 1.56%, while its palm oil contract gained 2.52%. Soyoil on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) added 1.93%.

* Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Oil prices jumped more than 9%, hitting their highest in months, after Israel said it struck targets in Iran, dramatically escalating tensions in the Middle East and raising worries about disrupted oil supplies.

* Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* India's palm oil imports in May rose about 84% month-on-month to 592,888 metric tons, a trade body said on Thursday.

* Malaysian ringgit, the palm's currency of trade, strengthened 0.55% against the U.S. dollar, making the contract more expensive for holders of foreign currencies.

* Palm oil may break resistance at 3,927 ringgit per metric ton and rise toward the 3,962-3,998 ringgit range, according to Reuters' technical analyst Wang Tao.

MARKET NEWS

Stocks dived in early Asian trade, led by a selloff in U.S. futures, while oil prices jumped after Israel conducted a military strike on Iran, sending investors scurrying to safe havens such as gold and the Swiss franc.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0600 Germany HICP Final YY May

0645 France CPI YY NSA May

0645 France CPI MM NSA May

0900 EU Total Trade Balance SA April

($1 = 4.2400 ringgit)

data.title: Pan-International to Acquire Factory Assets In Malaysia Worth Nearly MYR49 Million
data.description:
Pan-International Industrial board approved the proposed acquisition of factory assets in Malaysia from Sharp North Malaysia for up to 48.5 million ringgit.

The targeted assets include the land usage rights for plots 7 and PT8, as well as a building on Lot 202, all located in Kedah, Malaysia.

The land spans 690,500 square feet, while the building's floor area covers 578,904 square feet, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The final sale deal is contingent on local government approvals, the electronic components manufacturer added.

Shares dropped over 1% recently.
